pull_request.title: Mcp java
pull_request.body:
Move to mcp_java for annotations
Adding integration tests
Adding support for pagination, subscription and eliciation and several small fixes

changed_files.patch:
@@ -208,7 +208,8 @@ The feature pack can act as an MCP client with support for multiple transports:
 ## MCP Server Support
 
 The feature pack also supports exposing your Jakarta EE application as an MCP Server using the `mcp-server` Galleon layer.
-What you need to do in that case is to use the `org.wildfly:wildfly-mcp-api` artifact as a provided dependency and annotate the code you want to expose with the annotations provided by the API.
+What you need to do in that case is to use the `org.mcp-java:mcp-annotations` artifact as a provided dependency and annotate the code you want to expose with the annotations provided by the API.
+For more informations about `org.mcp-java:mcp-annotations` you can check [mcp_java](https://github.com/mcp-java/java-mcp-annotations).
 
 You may want to take a look at [wildfly-weather](https://github.com/ehsavoie/wildfly-weather) example.

@@ -0,0 +1,197 @@
+<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
+<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 http://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
+    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>
+
+    <parent>
+        <groupId>org.wildfly.generative-ai</groupId>
+        <artifactId>wildfly-ai-testsuite</artifactId>
+        <version>0.9.2-SNAPSHOT</version>
+    </parent>
+
+    <artifactId>wildfly-ai-testsuite-mcp</artifactId>
+    <packaging>jar</packaging>
+
+    <name>WildFly AI Feature Pack - MCP Integration Tests</name>
+    <description>Integration tests for WildFly MCP Server subsystem</description>
+
+    <properties>
+        <version.junit>5.11.4</version.junit>
+        <version.arquillian>1.10.0.Final</version.arquillian>
+        <version.wildfly.arquillian>5.0.1.Final</version.wildfly.arquillian>
+        <version.assertj>3.26.3</version.assertj>
+        <version.wildfly.maven.plugin>5.1.0.Final</version.wildfly.maven.plugin>
+        <version.maven.surefire>3.5.2</version.maven.surefire>
+        <jboss.home>${project.build.directory}/server</jboss.home>
+    </properties>
+
+    <dependencyManagement>
+        <dependencies>
+            <dependency>
+                <groupId>org.jboss.arquillian</groupId>
+                <artifactId>arquillian-bom</artifactId>
+                <version>${version.arquillian}</version>
+                <type>pom</type>
+                <scope>import</scope>
+            </dependency>
+            <dependency>
+                <groupId>org.junit</groupId>
+                <artifactId>junit-bom</artifactId>
+                <version>${version.junit}</version>
+                <type>pom</type>
+                <scope>import</scope>
+            </dependency>
+        </dependencies>
+    </dependencyManagement>
+
+    <dependencies>
+        <!-- JUnit 5 -->
+        <dependency>
+            <groupId>org.junit.jupiter</groupId>
+            <artifactId>junit-jupiter</artifactId>
+            <scope>test</scope>
+        </dependency>
+
+        <!-- Arquillian JUnit 5 -->
+        <dependency>
+            <groupId>org.jboss.arquillian.junit5</groupId>
+            <artifactId>arquillian-junit5-container</artifactId>
+            <scope>test</scope>
+        </dependency>
+
+        <!-- WildFly Arquillian Managed Container -->
+        <dependency>
+            <groupId>org.wildfly.arquillian</groupId>
+            <artifactId>wildfly-arquillian-container-managed</artifactId>
+            <version>${version.wildfly.arquillian}</version>
+            <scope>test</scope>
+        </dependency>
+
+        <!-- AssertJ for fluent assertions -->
+        <dependency>
+            <groupId>org.assertj</groupId>
+            <artifactId>assertj-core</artifactId>
+            <version>${version.assertj}</version>
+            <scope>test</scope>
+        </dependency>
+
+        <!-- Jakarta EE APIs (provided scope) -->
+        <dependency>
+            <groupId>jakarta.enterprise</groupId>
+            <artifactId>jakarta.enterprise.cdi-api</artifactId>
+            <scope>provided</scope>
+        </dependency>
+
+        <dependency>
+            <groupId>jakarta.inject</groupId>
+            <artifactId>jakarta.inject-api</artifactId>
+            <scope>provided</scope>
+        </dependency>
+
+        <!-- Jakarta JSON for response parsing in tests -->
+        <dependency>
+            <groupId>jakarta.json</groupId>
+            <artifactId>jakarta.json-api</artifactId>
+            <scope>test</scope>
+        </dependency>
+        <dependency>
+            <groupId>org.eclipse.parsson</groupId>
+            <artifactId>parsson</artifactId>
+            <scope>test</scope>
+        </dependency>
+
+        <!-- MCP annotations for test beans -->
+        <dependency>
+            <groupId>org.mcp-java</groupId>
+            <artifactId>mcp-annotations</artifactId>
+            <scope>provided</scope>
+        </dependency>
+        <dependency>
+            <groupId>org.mcp-java</groupId>
+            <artifactId>mcp-model</artifactId>
+            <scope>provided</scope>
+        </dependency>
+
+        <!-- WildFly MCP injection API (ElicitationSender, schema types, etc.) -->
+        <dependency>
+            <groupId>org.wildfly.generative-ai</groupId>
+            <artifactId>wildfly-mcp-injection</artifactId>
+            <scope>provided</scope>
+        </dependency>
+    </dependencies>
+
+    <build>
+        <testResources>
+            <testResource>
+                <directory>src/test/resources</directory>
+                <filtering>true</filtering>
+                <includes>
+                    <include>provisioning.xml</include>
+                </includes>
+            </testResource>
+            <testResource>
+                <directory>src/test/resources</directory>
+                <filtering>false</filtering>
+                <excludes>
+                    <exclude>provisioning.xml</exclude>
+                </excludes>
+            </testResource>
+        </testResources>
+        <plugins>
+            <!-- WildFly Maven Plugin - provision server with MCP support -->
+            <plugin>
+                <groupId>org.wildfly.plugins</groupId>
+                <artifactId>wildfly-maven-plugin</artifactId>
+                <version>${version.wildfly.maven.plugin}</version>
+                <executions>
+                    <execution>
+                        <id>provision-server</id>
+                        <phase>process-test-resources</phase>
+                        <goals>
+                            <goal>provision</goal>
+                        </goals>
+                        <configuration>
+                            <provisioning-file>${project.build.testOutputDirectory}/provisioning.xml</provisioning-file>
+                            <stability-level>experimental</stability-level>
+                            <galleon-options>
+                                <jboss-fork-embedded>true</jboss-fork-embedded>
+                            </galleon-options>
+                        </configuration>
+                    </execution>
+                </executions>
+            </plugin>
+
+            <!-- Maven Dependency Plugin - Copy test libraries for Arquillian deployments -->
+            <plugin>
+                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
+                <artifactId>maven-dependency-plugin</artifactId>
+                <executions>
+                    <execution>
+                        <id>copy-test-libs</id>
+                        <phase>process-test-classes</phase>
+                        <goals>
+                            <goal>copy</goal>
+                        </goals>
+                        <configuration>
+                            <artifactItems>
+                                <artifactItem>
+                                    <groupId>org.assertj</groupId>
+                                    <artifactId>assertj-core</artifactId>
+                                    <version>${version.assertj}</version>
+                                    <outputDirectory>${project.build.directory}/test-libs</outputDirectory>
+                                </artifactItem>
+                            </artifactItems>
+                        </configuration>
+                    </execution>
+                </executions>
+            </plugin>
+
+            <!-- Maven Surefire Plugin -->
+            <plugin>
+                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
+                <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
+                <version>${version.maven.surefire}</version>
+            </plugin>
+        </plugins>
+    </build>
+
+</project>

@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
+<arquillian xmlns="http://jboss.org/schema/arquillian"
+            xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
+            xsi:schemaLocation="http://jboss.org/schema/arquillian http://jboss.org/schema/arquillian/arquillian_1_0.xsd">
+
+    <container qualifier="wildfly-managed" default="true">
+        <configuration>
+            <property name="jbossHome">${jboss.home:target/server}</property>
+            <property name="serverConfig">standalone.xml</property>
+            <property name="managementAddress">127.0.0.1</property>
+            <property name="managementPort">9990</property>
+            <property name="startupTimeoutInSeconds">120</property>
+            <property name="allowConnectingToRunningServer">false</property>
+            <property name="jbossArguments">--stability=experimental</property>
+        </configuration>
+    </container>
+
+</arquillian>

@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@
+<?xml version="1.0" ?>
+<installation xmlns="urn:jboss:galleon:provisioning:4.0">
+    <feature-pack location="org.wildfly:wildfly-galleon-pack:${version.org.wildfly}">
+        <default-configs inherit="false"/>
+        <packages inherit="false"/>
+    </feature-pack>
+    <feature-pack location="org.wildfly.generative-ai:wildfly-ai-feature-pack:${project.version}">
+        <default-configs inherit="false"/>
+        <packages inherit="false"/>
+    </feature-pack>
+    <config model="standalone" name="standalone.xml">
+        <layers>
+            <!-- Base WildFly layers -->
+            <include name="cdi"/>
+            <include name="jaxrs"/>
+            <include name="management"/>
+
+            <!-- MCP Server layer -->
+            <include name="mcp-server"/>
+        </layers>
+    </config>
+    <options>
+        <option name="jboss-fork-embedded" value="true"/>
+        <option name="optional-packages" value="passive+"/>
+    </options>
+</installation>

@@ -16,6 +16,7 @@
 
     <modules>
         <module>integration</module>
+        <module>mcp</module>
     </modules>
 
 </project>

@@ -1,22 +0,0 @@

-<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 http://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">

@@ -1,45 +0,0 @@ -/* - * Copyright The WildFly Authors - * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 - */ -package org.wildfly.mcp.api; - -import static java.lang.annotation.ElementType.METHOD; -import static java.lang.annotation.RetentionPolicy.RUNTIME; - -import java.lang.annotation.Retention; -import java.lang.annotation.Target; -import java.util.List; - -/** - * Annotates a business method of a CDI bean used to complete a prompt argument - *

- * The result of a "complete" operation is always represented as a {@link CompletionResponse}. However, the annotated method can - * also return other types that are converted according to the following rules. - *

    - *
  • If the method returns {@link String} then the response contains the single value.
    • - *
  • If the method returns a {@link List} of {@link String}s then the response contains the list of values.
    • - *
  • The method may return a {@link Uni} that wraps any of the type mentioned above.
    • - *
- * In other words, the return type must be one of the following list: - *
    - *
  • {@code CompletionResponse}
    • - *
  • {@code String}
    • - *
  • {@code List}
    • - *
  • {@code Uni}
    • - *
  • {@code Uni}
    • - *
  • {@code Uni>}
    • - *
- * - * @see Prompt#name() - */ -@Retention(RUNTIME) -@Target(METHOD) -public @interface CompletePrompt { - - /** - * The name reference to a prompt. If not such {@link Prompt} exists then the build fails. - */ - String value(); - -}

@@ -1,53 +0,0 @@ -package org.wildfly.mcp.api; - -import static java.lang.annotation.ElementType.METHOD; -import static java.lang.annotation.RetentionPolicy.RUNTIME; - -import java.lang.annotation.Retention; -import java.lang.annotation.Target; -import java.util.List; -/** - * Annotates a business method of a CDI bean as an exposed prompt template. - *

- * The result of a "prompt get" operation is always represented as a {@link PromptResponse}. However, the annotated method can - * also return other types that are converted according to the following rules. - *

    - *
  • If the method returns a {@link PromptMessage} then the response has no description and contains the single - * message object.
    • - *

@@ -0,0 +1,47 @@ +/* + * Copyright The WildFly Authors + * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 + */ +package org.wildfly.extension.mcp.injection.elicitation; + +/** + * Injected into tool methods to allow pausing execution and requesting additional + * user input from the MCP client. + * + *

The client must declare the {@code "elicitation"} capability during initialization. + * Use {@link #isSupported()} to check before calling {@link #send}.

+ * + *

Example tool method signature:

+ * 
{@code
+ * @Tool(description = "Creates a user account")
+ * public String createAccount(String email, ElicitationSender elicitation) throws Exception {
+ *     if (elicitation.isSupported()) {
+ *         ElicitationResponse response = elicitation.send(
+ *             ElicitationRequest.builder("Please confirm the account details")
+ *                 .addSchemaProperty("confirm", new BooleanSchema(true))
+ *                 .build());
+ *         if (!response.isAccepted()) return "Cancelled";
+ *     }
+ *     // proceed...
+ * }
+ * }
+ */ +public interface ElicitationSender { + + /** + * Send an elicitation request to the client and block until the client responds + * or the timeout (default 30 s) expires. + * + * @param request the request describing the message and schema + * @return the client's response + * @throws IllegalStateException if the client does not support elicitation + * @throws java.util.concurrent.TimeoutException if the client does not respond within the timeout + * @throws InterruptedException if the calling thread is interrupted while waiting + */ + ElicitationResponse send(ElicitationRequest request) throws Exception; + + /** + * Returns {@code true} if the connected client declared the {@code "elicitation"} capability. + */ + boolean isSupported(); +}

@@ -0,0 +1,22 @@
+/*
+ * Copyright The WildFly Authors
+ * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
+ */
+package org.wildfly.extension.mcp.injection.elicitation;
+
+import jakarta.json.JsonObjectBuilder;
+
+/**
+ * Marker interface for MCP elicitation schema property types.
+ * Each implementation serializes itself to JSON Schema via {@link #asJson()}.
+ */
+public interface PrimitiveSchema {
+
+    boolean required();
+
+    /**
+     * Returns a {@link JsonObjectBuilder} containing the JSON Schema representation
+     * of this property. The caller is responsible for calling {@code .build()}.
+     */
+    JsonObjectBuilder asJson();
+}

commit_summaries.message:
refactor: Replace wildfly-mcp/api module with java-mcp-annotations library

Remove the custom wildfly-mcp-api module and adopt the shared
org.mcp-java annotations and model library (mcp-annotations, mcp-model).
This aligns wildfly-mcp with the same annotation API used by
quarkus-mcp-server.

Key changes:
- Delete wildfly-mcp/api/ module (18 source files)
- Update annotation imports to org.mcp_java.annotations.*
- Add @ResourceTemplate + @ResourceTemplateArg scanning
- Port ContentMapper to subsystem using new model types
  (ContentBlock, PromptMessage, ResourceContents)
- Rewrite ResourceMessageHandler for single-record ResourceContents
- Root pom.xml: add version property, update dependency management
- bom/pom.xml: replace wildfly-mcp-api with mcp-annotations + mcp-model
- ai-feature-pack/pom.xml: replace dependency
- ai-feature-pack module.xml: replace artifact references for JBoss Modules
- wildfly-wasm/injection/pom.xml: remove unused wildfly-mcp-api dependency
- wildfly-mcp/subsystem/pom.xml: use managed version instead of hardcoded
- README.md: update artifact reference in documentation

Signed-off-by: Emmanuel Hugonnet <ehugonne@redhat.com>

feat(mcp): add MCP server integration with testing framework

Implement comprehensive MCP server functionality including:
- Integration test suite with 383-line test case
- Completion and resource template handlers
- Refactored message handlers for prompts, tools, and resources
- CDI integration via WildFlyMCPRegistry
- Enhanced dependency processing
- Fixed annotation response errors

Signed-off-by: Emmanuel Hugonnet <ehugonne@redhat.com>

fix(mcp): validate JSON-RPC message params are present before processing

Guard against null or non-object params in toolsCall, promptsGet,
resourceCall and resourceTemplateRead to return INVALID_PARAMS instead
of throwing a NullPointerException on malformed requests.

Signed-off-by: Emmanuel Hugonnet <ehugonne@redhat.com>

feat(mcp): report subscribe capability and handle resources/subscribe and resources/unsubscribe

Add "subscribe": true to the resources capability in the initialize
response, and add handlers for resources/subscribe and
resources/unsubscribe that acknowledge the request. Resource change
notifications are not yet implemented.
dding tests to cover that new feature.

Signed-off-by: Emmanuel Hugonnet <ehugonne@redhat.com>

feat(mcp): validate tool names against the MCP spec pattern on registration

Tool names must match ^[A-Za-z0-9_.\-]{1,128}$ as recommended by the
MCP spec. WildFlyMCPRegistry.addTool() now throws IllegalArgumentException
for non-conforming names. Covered by ToolNameValidationTestCase.

Signed-off-by: Emmanuel Hugonnet <ehugonne@redhat.com>

test(mcp): verify AudioContent is correctly encoded in tool responses

AudioContent implements ContentBlock, so it flows through ContentMapper
and is serialized by Jackson to the correct MCP JSON structure
(type/data/mimeType). The tests document this behavior and guard against
regressions where audio content could be accidentally converted to text.

Signed-off-by: Emmanuel Hugonnet <ehugonne@redhat.com>

feat(mcp): add cursor-based pagination for list endpoints

Adds MCP spec-compliant pagination to tools/list, prompts/list,
resources/list and resources/templates/list. Items are sorted by name
for stable ordering; a base64-encoded cursor carries the last-seen item
name across requests. Page size is configurable via the page-size
attribute on the mcp-server subsystem element (default 0 = disabled).

Signed-off-by: Emmanuel Hugonnet <ehugonne@redhat.com>

feat(mcp): implement full elicitation request/response flow

Elicitation API (injection module):
- Add PrimitiveSchema interface and concrete schema types: StringSchema,
  BooleanSchema, NumberSchema, IntegerSchema, EnumSchema
- Add ElicitationRequest value class with builder (validates at least one
  schema property is provided, configurable timeout, default 30 s)
- Add ElicitationResponse value class with Action enum (ACCEPT, DECLINE, CANCEL)
  and typed accessors (getString, getBoolean, getInteger, getNumber, getStrings)
- Add ElicitationSender interface to be injected into tool methods

Elicitation runtime (subsystem):
- Add PendingRequestRegistry: tracks server-initiated requests via
  CompletableFuture; ID coercion handles both JSON number and string ids
- Add ElicitationSenderImpl: sends elicitation/create, blocks the tool thread
  until the client responds (or times out with cleanup), parses the result
- Add ToolMessageHandler.buildArguments(): injects ElicitationSenderImpl for
  ElicitationSender-typed parameters, delegates to prepareArguments for others
- Route client responses (result/error messages) to PendingRequestRegistry in
  MCPMessageHandler instead of discarding them
- Filter ElicitationSender parameters from the tools/list inputSchema
- Advertise elicitation capability in server capabilities on initialize
- Fix MCPServerDependencyProcessor.processTools() to iterate all method
  parameters in declaration order, not just @ToolArg-annotated ones, so that
  ElicitationSender parameters are included in argumentTypes() and the
  MethodHandle resolves to the correct signature

Tests:
- 39 unit tests: schema JSON serialization, PendingRequestRegistry round-trips
  and ID coercion, ElicitationSenderImpl isSupported/send/timeout, and
  MCPMessageHandler response routing and capability advertisement
- 4 integration tests: elicitation tool listed without ElicitationSender in
  inputSchema, full accept/decline round-trips, mixed regular args + elicitation

Signed-off-by: Emmanuel Hugonnet <ehugonne@redhat.com>
